mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 6.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment rate above 6.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and increases risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in housing starts above 1.1 million signals a strengthening housing market that increases consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising housing starts above 1.1 million are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in payroll employment above 140,000 signals a strengthening labor market that reduces unemployment and increases consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising payroll employment above 140,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in real GDP above 3.5% signals a strengthening economy that increases corporate profitability and reduces risk premia, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP above 3.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising federal funds rate above 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in industrial production above 102 signals a strengthening supply chain that increases corporate profitability and reduces risk premia, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising industrial production above 102 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction between INDPRO and HOUST does not contribute to the signal (partial correlation p > 0.05).
